newConcepts:
# CONCEPT 1
**Text description:** Show founders how to use a free AI tool to instantly identify the top 3 conversion-killing issues on their website — then fix them in minutes, without hiring a developer.

## HOOK
**What is seen in the first 2 seconds:** A split screen. Left side: a slow-loading, cluttered website with a red overlay saying “3 seconds load time — Bounce rate +50%”. Right side: the same website after a quick audit, now clean and fast, with a green overlay saying “1.2 seconds — Conversion +40%”. A phone bar across the top shows the video is recording.

**What is said in the first line:**  
“Your website is leaking leads every single second — and you don’t even know where the leak is.”

**Why this hook works specifically for founders & coaches 25–45 who feel their website underperforms:**  
It directly names the pain (underperformance) and frames it as an *invisible problem* — not a lack of design, but a hidden technical or UX leak. The split-screen contrast immediately shows a measurable result, creating urgency and curiosity. Founders who have spent money on a site that “just doesn’t work” will stop scrolling to learn the fix.

## SCRIPT
- [0:00–0:03] **Hook.** Split screen as described. Calm, direct voiceover: “Your website is leaking leads every single second — and you don’t even know where the leak is.”
- [0:03–0:06] **Transition to screen recording.** Full screen recording of a browser tab. “I’m going to show you the exact 60-second AI audit I run for every client before I build their Framer site.”
- [0:06–0:10] **Step 1: Run the AI tool.** Finger opens a new tab, types a URL (a generic client website), and opens Google PageSpeed Insights. “First, grab your website URL and open PageSpeed Insights. This is free, and it gives you a raw performance score.”
- [0:10–0:14] **Step 2: Use AI to interpret.** Opens ChatGPT or a custom GPT. Pastes the PageSpeed report text. “But raw data is useless — so I paste the report into a custom GPT I built, which highlights the three issues that actually hurt conversions.”
- [0:14–0:20] **Step 3: The three issues.** Screen shows ChatGPT’s output: 1) Unoptimized images, 2) Missing alt tags, 3) Render-blocking scripts. “It spits out three fixes, prioritized by impact. Most founders miss these because they look technical — but the fix is actually simple.”
- [0:20–0:26] **Step 4: Quick fix demonstration.** Cuts to a Framer interface. Quickly shows how to compress an image (drag-and-drop a compressed version) and add alt text. “For images: compress them before uploading. For alt text: describe the image in natural language — Google uses it for SEO. And for render-blocking, a simple Framer plugin handles it in one click.”
- [0:26–0:30] **Result reveal.** Cuts back to the split screen from the hook, now with the improved metrics. “These three changes took me three minutes. The result? A site that loads in under 1.5 seconds and keeps visitors engaged.”
- [0:30–0:34] **Soft CTA.** Talking head returns, calm smile. “I run this audit on every site I build. If you want the same done for yours, comment ‘AUDIT’ and I’ll send you a free, no-strings scan.”  
  *On-screen text: “Comment AUDIT”*

**Teaching takeaway:** Founders learn that website performance issues are not mysterious — a free AI tool and three simple fixes can immediately improve load time and conversion rate.

**Subtle authority:** The voiceover uses “I do this for every client” and “I built a custom GPT” — teaching freely, positioning as the expert who systematizes the process.

---

# CONCEPT 2
**Text description:** Teach founders how to use an AI prompt to rewrite their hero section from a description of services into a lead-generating statement of value — with a before/after example.

## HOOK
**What is seen in the first 2 seconds:** A split screen. Left side: a website hero that says “We offer web design and development services. Contact us for a quote.” Right side: the same site with a new hero: “Get 3x more leads with a website that books calls while you sleep.” A subtle arrow points from old to new. Text overlay: “Same business. Different words. Different results.”

**What is said in the first line:**  
“Your hero section is probably just describing what you do — that’s why nobody clicks.”

**Why this hook works specifically for founders & coaches 25–45 who feel their website underperforms:**  
It directly challenges a common mistake: founders treat their hero like an “about us” page instead of a value proposition. The side-by-side comparison instantly makes the problem visible. The hook implies the fix is simple — changing words — which feels low-effort and high-leverage.

## SCRIPT
- [0:00–0:03] **Hook.** Split screen as described. Voiceover (calm, confident): “Your hero section is probably just describing what you do — that’s why nobody clicks.”
- [0:03–0:07] **Explain the mistake.** Talking head, simple background. “Most founders write: ‘We build websites for businesses.’ But your visitor doesn’t care what you do — they care what your service does *for them*.”
- [0:07–0:12] **Introduce the AI tool.** Screen recording of ChatGPT. “I use a simple AI formula to turn a service description into a benefit-driven hero. Let me show you.”
- [0:12–0:18] **Show the prompt.** Typing into ChatGPT: “Act as a high-conversion copywriter. Rewrite this hero section for a web design agency. Focus on the outcome for the founder, not the process. Use: [original text]. Keep it under 10 words for the headline, 20 for subhead.”  
  “The magic is in the prompt — it forces the AI to focus on *results*, not features.”
- [0:18–0:22] **AI response.** ChatGPT returns a headline: “Convert visitors into clients with a site that works 24/7.” Subhead: “Stop losing leads to a slow, confusing website. Get a Framer site optimized for conversions in 2 weeks.”
- [0:22–0:26] **Apply to Framer.** Quick cut to Framer interface, copying and pasting the new hero text. “I paste that directly into my Framer site — takes 30 seconds.”
- [0:26–0:30] **Result demonstration.** Shows a mockup of the before/after hero with a heatmap overlay. Left side: no clicks on CTA. Right side: CTA glowing with clicks. “The same design, same business — just different words. The new hero got 3x more click-throughs on a client’s site last month.”
- [0:30–0:34] **Soft CTA.** Talking head again. “If you want me to rewrite your hero section using this exact AI formula, comment ‘FORMULA’ and I’ll send you the prompt and a free one-line rewrite.”  
  *On-screen text: “Comment FORMULA”*

**Teaching takeaway:** Founders learn that copy is often the bottleneck, not design. They can immediately apply this prompt to any part of their site.

**Subtle authority:** The heatmap result and client example show real-world proof. The offer of a “free one-line rewrite” feels generous and builds trust.

---

# CONCEPT 3
**Text description:** Demonstrate how to turn a one-line client testimonial into a full, persuasive case study using an AI framework — then embed it on the website for instant credibility.

## HOOK
**What is seen in the first 2 seconds:** A split screen. Left: a boring testimonial block with “Great work! Highly recommend.” Right: a structured case study with “Problem → Solution → Result” and a specific metric (e.g., “+150% leads in 3 months”). A cursor moves from left to right. Overlay text: “Same client. Same result. 10x more persuasive.”

**What is said in the first line:**  
“A single line of praise doesn’t sell. A structured story does — and you can build one in 5 minutes with AI.”

**Why this hook works specifically for founders & coaches 25–45 who feel their website underperforms:**  
Founders often have good testimonials but present them weakly — a block quote with no context. The hook highlights the gap between “okay social proof” and “compelling evidence.” It promises a quick AI-driven fix that transforms an asset they already have.

## SCRIPT
- [0:00–0:03] **Hook.** Split screen as described. Voiceover: “A single line of praise doesn’t sell. A structured story does — and you can build one in 5 minutes with AI.”
- [0:03–0:07] **Context.** Talking head. “You probably have a folder of testimonials from happy clients. But if you just paste them on your site as quotes, you’re leaving 90% of their impact on the table.”
- [0:07–0:12] **Show the raw material.** Screen recording showing a simple text file with client quote: “Aamir redesigned our site. Our lead flow doubled. Amazing work.” “Here’s a real testimonial. Short, nice — but not a story.”
- [0:12–0:18] **Introduce AI case study builder.** Opens ChatGPT with a specific prompt. “I use this prompt: ‘Take this client testimonial and expand it into a case study with three sections: Problem — before working with us, Solution — what we did, Result — specific numbers. Use client’s language. Keep it under 150 words.’”
- [0:18–0:24] **AI output.** ChatGPT returns a mini case study with headings. Example: “Problem: Our website wasn’t converting visitors. We were spending money on ads but getting no calls. Solution: Aamir redesigned with a clear CTA and fast-loading pages. Result: Within 3 months, our lead flow doubled — from 10 to 25 per week.”  
  “Notice how the AI keeps the client’s original compliment but adds structure and a specific number.”
- [0:24–0:28] **Show embedding.** Quick cut to Framer interface, pasting the case study into a dedicated “Results” section. “I add a ‘Results’ section to my site and drop these in. One per client. It takes 2 minutes.”
- [0:28–0:32] **Result reveal.** Shows a full-page mockup of the case study on a mobile and desktop view. “Now when a potential client lands on your site, they see proof — not just praise. That’s what gets them to reach out.”
- [0:32–0:36] **Soft CTA.** Talking head returns. “I use this framework for every client site I build. Comment ‘REVAMP’ and I’ll send you the exact prompt and a template to use today.”  
  *On-screen text: “Comment REVAMP”*

**Teaching takeaway:** Founders learn to transform existing testimonials into structured, persuasive case studies using AI — no extra interviews needed.

**Subtle authority:** The “I do this for every client” phrase and the free prompt/template offer reinforce expertise without a hard sell.

## CONCEPT 1

**First 2 seconds:** Split screen. Left side: a generic, ugly AI-generated website (blurry images, stock icons, messy layout). Right side: a polished, professional Framer homepage (clean typography, custom banner, smooth animations). You are standing between them, arms crossed, calm.

### SCRIPT
- **0:00–0:04 (Hook):** Split screen. Left = generic AI site, Right = your Framer build. You speak: *"Founders stop outsourcing your website to AI and then wondering why it looks like everyone else's."*

---

## CONCEPT 2

**First 2 seconds:** You are standing in front of a laptop screen showing a cluttered, 7-page website (counterintuitive). You press one key, and it compresses into a single, elegant scrolling page. Text overlay: "1 page > 7 pages."

**Why it works:** This directly contradicts a common founder mistake—thinking "more pages = more professional." Your ICP (bootstrapped founders, coaches) often overcomplicate their site. You’re giving them a permission slip to simplify, which feels like a relief.

### SCRIPT
- **0:00–0:03 (Hook):** You stand next to a screen showing a 7-page site. *"Your website has 7 pages. It should have 1."* You click, and the site collapses into a single scrolling page.  
- **0:04–0:06 (Transition):** *"Here’s the 3-section formula for a single-page site that converts."*  
- **0:07–0:18 (Section 1 – Trust):** Screen recording of a Framer canvas. *"Section 1: Social proof and results—immediately. Not 'About Us.' I use a client logo bar and a 1-line testimonial. No fluff. You have 3 seconds to prove you’re legit."*

“The problem isn’t the design. It’s the sample size. You’ve had 45 visitors. That’s a warm-up, not a race.”  

**Scene 3:** B-roll of a coffee shop or desk with a notebook.  
“Before you judge your website’s performance, get to 300–500 targeted visitors first. Then, and only then, can you say something is working or not.”  

**Scene 4:** Back to talking head, holding a phone.  
“One client last week had the same site for 2 months. No leads. We ran one simple audit. Turns out, their hero section had zero clarity. I fixed it in 20 minutes. Now they’re getting 3 calls a week.”  

**CTA:**  
“If your site has traffic but no conversions, send ‘AUDIT’ and I’ll show you the one thing I’d change first.”  

---

## CONCEPT 2

**Text description:**  
Many founders buy expensive templates and tools expecting instant results. This video challenges the “shiny object” trap and teaches a simple, volume-based approach to building a website that actually converts.

### HOOK
- **First 2 seconds:**  
  A split screen: left side shows a complex, expensive Framer template with dozens of sections; right side shows a simple, clean 3-page version. The left side is faded, the right side is highlighted.  
- **Spoken first line:**  
  “You bought the most expensive template on the market… and you’re still waiting for your first client.”  
- **Why it works:**  
  Founders love tools (Framer, Webflow, etc.) but often overcomplicate. This hook challenges the belief that “more features = more results.” It shows Aamir knows the real formula is simplicity + volume.

### SCRIPT
**Scene 1:** Talking head, slight smile.  
“You spent 4 hours building a Framer site with 12 sections, 3 animations, and a custom cursor. Your first client still hasn’t come.”  

**Scene 2:** Screen record of a real client’s before/after site.  
“Here’s the truth: your website is a tool, not a trophy. It needs to work, not win awards.”  

**Scene 3:** B-roll of a hand writing “500 visitors” on a whiteboard.  
“I tell every founder I work with: get 500 genuine visitors to your site before you even think about a redesign. Then, you can see what’s actually broken.”  

**Scene 4:** Back to talking head, calm.  
“One client last week had 200 visits and 0 conversions. The issue? Their CTA was buried. We moved it to the top. Within 48 hours, they got their first lead.”  

**CTA:**  
“If you’ve built a site but aren’t seeing results, send ‘FORMULA’ and I’ll share the 3-page framework I use for every client.”  

---

## CONCEPT 3

**Text description:**  
Founders often send traffic to their site without understanding the real bottleneck. This video teaches a simple “volume before judgment” rule that saves them from wasting money on redesigns.

### HOOK
- **First 2 seconds:**  
  A screenshot of a Google Analytics page showing “Bounce Rate: 87%” and “Sessions: 12”. A finger points to the data.  
- **Spoken first line:**  
  “You’re worried about your bounce rate… with 12 visitors. Let’s talk.”  
- **Why it works:**  
  This hook directly challenges the founder’s anxiety about their site “failing.” It reframes the problem as a lack of data, not a lack of quality. It shows Aamir has the authority to demystify analytics for them.

### SCRIPT
**Scene 1:** Talking head, dry humor.  
“I see it every week: a founder sends me a screenshot of their analytics saying ‘my site is broken’… and they’ve had 19 visitors.”  

**Scene 2:** Screen record of a real Framer site with low traffic.  
“You can’t diagnose a problem with 19 data points. That’s not a diagnosis. That’s a guess.”  

**Scene 3:** B-roll of a hand drawing a simple funnel on a whiteboard.  
“Here’s the rule: before you touch your site’s design, get 500 people to visit it. Then, look for patterns. One person clicking the CTA? Good. 500 people clicking nothing? Then you know where to start.”  

**Scene 4:** Back to talking head, warm tone.  
“Last week, a coach had 300 visits and 2 clicks. We ran a quick revamp on their hero section. Now, they’re getting 12 clicks per 100 visits. Same site, smarter copy.”  

**CTA:**  
“If you’ve got traffic but no conversions, send ‘REVAMP’ and I’ll give you one tweak that’ll change everything.”

analysis:
# CONCEPT
Figma has launched new AI features that promise to revolutionize how designers work, challenging the perception that previous AI design tools were impractical. The video showcases these new features, demonstrating their real-world utility and efficiency.

# HOOK
The hook is fast-paced and declarative, immediately grabbing attention with a bold claim.
- **VISUAL:** A man directly addresses the camera with animated hand gestures, then quickly turns to his desk, sitting down in front of a monitor, followed by a rapid cut to a screen displaying Figma's interface. Bright, contrasting colors briefly flash in the background later.
- **TEXT:** "Figma just changed how designers are gonna work." This is then broken down into single words: "DESIGNERS," "WORK," "NEW."
- **AUDIO:** "Figma just changed how designers are gonna work because these new AI features by Figma are actually insane."
The hook creates strong curiosity and identity relevance for designers, promising an "insane" shift in their workflow.

# RETENTION MECHANISMS
The video uses rapid cuts, text overlays, and a clear demonstration-style narrative to maintain engagement.
- **Micro-escalations:** Features are introduced sequentially, each building on the last, with visual cues (e.g., UI elements animating into existence) and enthusiastic vocal delivery.
- **Pattern interrupts:** A quick, bright yellow screen flash with abstract shapes breaks the visual monotony between feature demonstrations, signaling a shift to the next "mind-blowing" aspect. Text overlays with keywords emphasize key points.
- **Clear forward momentum:** The creator explicitly states he's "testing" the features and walks through three distinct use cases, ensuring the viewer understands the journey and anticipates the next reveal. The main insight (AI understanding workflow) is delayed until the end.

# REWARD
The viewer is rewarded with a clear understanding of Figma's new, practical AI capabilities. This provides education on how these tools can streamline design workflows, potentially saving significant time and effort. It inspires designers to consider how AI can genuinely augment their process rather than just generating superficial outputs.

# SCRIPT
1.  **[0:00-0:04]** (Man in plaid shirt, glasses, speaking directly to camera, animated hand gestures) "Figma just changed how designers are gonna work because these new AI features by Figma are actually insane." (Visual: text "Figma just changed how designers are gonna work" then "DESIGNERS", "WORK", "NEW")
2.  **[0:05-0:15]** (Man sits at desk, looking at monitor, then screen shows Figma UI with AI prompt box) "So I partnered with Figma to test their new AI agent before launch. And no, this isn't one of those AI tools that just gives you pretty screens you can't even use properly after." (Visual: "AGENT", "LAUNCH", "AI TOOLS" text overlays, mouse cursor clicks "Send")
3.  **[0:15-0:29]** (Close up of man's face, then back to Figma UI) "This feels way more real and practical. So first, I gave Figma one simple screen, just a basic login page, and it literally turned it into a full app flow in seconds. Sign-up screens, OTP screens, welcome pages, and more. Like, the design just kept expanding." (Visual: "REAL", "PRACTICAL", "SIMPLE", "BASIC" text overlays. Figma UI animates creating multiple connected app screens. "APP FLOW IN SECONDS", "SCREENS", "AND MORE" text overlays appear as screens generate.)
4.  **[0:29-0:37]** (Figma UI shows editing text fields) "And the best part, I could still click and edit everything normally inside Figma after. That's really nice. But wait till you see this next one." (Visual: "BEST PART", "EDIT EVERYTHING NORMALLY", "FIGMA AFTER", "THIS NEXT ONE" text overlays)
5.  **[0:37-0:54]** (Transition to yellow background with shapes, then Figma UI with a wireframe) "Later, I took a basic ugly wireframe and asked it to use my design system to match my app style. And in seconds, it pulls every component, every token, every text style from my library to match my app style. Usually, this stuff takes so long to fix manually. Here, it just worked like a charm." (Visual: "UGLY WIREFRAME", "MY DESIGN SYSTEM", "APP STYLE" text overlays. Wireframe transforms into a polished dark mode UI. "COMPONENT", "TOKEN", "TEXT STYLE", "FROM MY LIBRARY", "APP STYLE" text overlays as elements are highlighted. "SO LONG MANUALLY", "CHARM" text overlays.)
6.  **[0:54-1:02]** (Back to man speaking) "And honestly, this is the first time AI inside design tools actually feels useful to me. Okay, but this third feature, this one really shocked me." (Visual: Yellow flash with lines. "USEFUL TO ME", "THIRD FEATURE", "REALLY SHOCKED ME" text overlays.)
7.  **[1:02-1:16]** (Figma UI with many comment pins) "You know when your design file has like 60 plus comments and everyone wants different changes? I asked Figma's AI to read everything and tell me what actually mattered. And it literally turned all the chaos into clear action points." (Visual: "60+ COMMENTS", "EVERYONE WANTS DIFFERENT", "ASKED FIGMA'S AI TO", "TELL ME WHAT ACTUALLY MATTERED" text overlays. AI generates a summarized list of feedback. "THE CHAOS", "CLEAR ACTION POINTS" text overlays.)
8.  **[1:16-1:24]** (Back to man speaking) "That's insane! Because the AI isn't generating screens anymore, it's understanding the workflow around design itself. And I think that's the biggest thing here." (Visual: "INSANE", "AI ISN'T GENERATING SCREENS", "IT'S UNDERSTANDING THE WORKFLOW", "DESIGN ITSELF", "BIGGEST THING HERE" text overlays.)
9.  **[1:24-1:39]** (Background changes to a list of features) "And this is just scratching the surface. You can also make bulk edits across screens, explore multiple layout directions, instantly generate realistic content and images, remix existing designs, and clean up repetitive work way faster. All directly inside Figma." (Visual: "SCRATCHING THE SURFACE" text. "BULK EDITS", "MULTIPLE LAYOUTS", "REAL CONTENT", "CLEAN UP", "USE STYLES", "USE LIBRARIES", "REMIX DESIGNS", "UPDATE CONTENT", "INSTANTLY" text overlays flash on screen, then man looking at laptop. "REPETITIVE WORK", "FASTER", "DIRECTLY INSIDE FIGMA" text overlays.)

newConcepts:
Here are 3 new video concepts tailored for your brand as **Aamir**, the 22-year-old founder of a web design & creative agency. The core of each concept is adapted from the original’s “AI changes the workflow” idea, but translated into **web design, AI, and client acquisition** — with a calm, authoritative tone that makes founders feel “I need this guy to build my site.”

---

## CONCEPT 1

**Text description:**  
Most founders think a “good website” means a pretty homepage. You show them that a high-converting site is actually a **systems-driven funnel** — and that AI can now map out that entire funnel from a single prompt.

### HOOK
- **Visual (first 2 seconds):** A clean screen recording of a simple landing page with one CTA button (“Get Started”). The screen then quickly zooms out to reveal a full, connected flow of 5+ pages (Pricing, Features, Onboarding, Dashboard).  
- **Spoken first line:** *“Most founders think a website is just a homepage. But a homepage that doesn’t lead to a system? That’s just a pretty poster, not a business.”*  
- **Why it works:** Your target audience (founders 25-45) often feel their site “looks good” but doesn’t *do* anything. This hook challenges the belief that a website = a single page, and frames the real value as a *system* — which you, Aamir, build.

### SCRIPT
1. **[0:00 – 0:04]** (Screen recording: a clean, minimal landing page with one CTA. Text overlay: “HOMEPAGE ≠ BUSINESS.”)  
   **Voiceover:** *“Most founders think a website is just a homepage. But a homepage that doesn’t lead to a system? That’s just a pretty poster, not a business.”*  
2. **[0:04 – 0:12]** (Screen zooms out to show a full funnel of 5+ connected pages — Pricing, Features, Onboarding, Dashboard. Text overlay: “SYSTEM.”)  
   **Voiceover:** *“I used an AI agent inside Framer to turn one single CTA button into a full client onboarding flow. From first click to payment — in seconds.”*  
3. **[0:12 – 0:25]** (Talking head, calm, direct eye contact. Text overlay: “AI DIDN’T JUST DESIGN. IT ARCHITECTED.”)  
   **Voiceover:** *“The AI didn’t just generate a pretty screen. It understood that a founder’s site needs to move a visitor from ‘interested’ to ‘client.’ It built the logic, the pages, the tokens — all connected.”*  
4. **[0:25 – 0:35]** (Screen recording shows the AI agent generating a “Client Path” — a visual flow of pages with decision points. Text overlay: “ONE PROMPT. FULL FUNNEL.”)  
   **Voiceover:** *“I gave it one prompt: ‘Build a system that turns a free audit into a paid client.’ And it gave me a complete funnel. That’s not just design — that’s strategy.”*  
5. **[0:35 – 0:42]** (Talking head, slight smile. Text overlay: “THE WEBSITE SHOULD BE YOUR SALES ENGINE.”)  
   **Voiceover:** *“Your website shouldn’t just look good. It should work like a machine that scales your time. That’s what I build for founders.”*  
6. **[0:42 – 0:50]** (CTA screen: “Comment ‘AUDIT’ to get a free funnel map for your site.”)  
   **Voiceover:** *“If you want to see if your site is actually a system, comment ‘AUDIT’ — I’ll show you what’s missing.”*

**Takeaway:** A website isn’t a page — it’s a process. Your CTA should be the start of a system, not the end of a thought.  
**CTA:** **AUDIT**

---

## CONCEPT 2

**Text description:**  
Founders waste hours on “content updates” that should take seconds. You show them how AI can read their entire site, understand their intent, and apply a full content overhaul — without breaking any design rules.

### HOOK
- **Visual (first 2 seconds):** A screen recording of a messy Figma/Framer file with dozens of comments, red annotations, and “Change this font” / “Move this button” notes everywhere. The screen then *animates* as the AI collapses all notes into a single, clean action list.  
- **Spoken first line:** *“If your designer sends you a file with 50 comments, you don’t have a design problem — you have a communication problem. I fixed it with one AI command.”*  
- **Why it works:** Founders hate “design feedback loops.” This hook immediately addresses the pain of long revision cycles and positions AI (and you) as the solution.

### SCRIPT
1. **[0:00 – 0:04]** (Screen: a messy Framer file with 50+ comments and red text. Text overlay: “50 COMMENTS.”)  
   **Voiceover:** *“If your designer sends you a file with 50 comments, you don’t have a design problem — you have a communication problem.”*  
2. **[0:04 – 0:12]** (Screen: AI agent processes all comments, turns them into a single “Action list.” Text overlay: “CHAOS → CLARITY.”)  
   **Voiceover:** *“I asked the AI inside Framer to read every single comment and tell me what actually mattered. It collapsed 50 requests into 4 clear actions.”*  
3. **[0:12 – 0:22]** (Talking head, calm. Text overlay: “THE AI UNDERSTOOD INTENT, NOT JUST WORDS.”)  
   **Voiceover:** *“It understood the intent behind each comment. ‘Make it more premium’? It pulled my font tokens. ‘Make it faster’? It optimized the layout. The AI didn’t just edit — it understood.”*  
4. **[0:22 – 0:30]** (Screen: before/after of a content block — same design, but cleaner, more aligned. Text overlay: “SAME DESIGN. BETTER EXECUTION.”)  
   **Voiceover:** *“The result? The same design you already loved — but cleaned up, polished, and ready to convert. No back-and-forth. No waiting.”*  
5. **[0:30 – 0:39]** (Talking head, direct. Text overlay: “YOU DON’T NEED MORE REVISIONS. YOU NEED BETTER TOOLS.”)  
   **Voiceover:** *“This is why I use AI in every project I build. It doesn’t replace the designer — it removes the noise. So the client gets what they actually wanted, faster.”*  
6. **[0:39 – 0:47]** (CTA screen: “Comment ‘REVAMP’ to get your site’s feedback compressed into 3 actionable steps.”)  
   **Voiceover:** *“If your site has a pile of comments you don’t know what to do with, comment ‘REVAMP’ — I’ll show you the real priorities.”*

**Takeaway:** More feedback ≠ better design. Let AI do the sorting so you can focus on what actually matters.  
**CTA:** **REVAMP**

---

## CONCEPT 3

**Text description:**  
Founders think they need to “learn design” to own their site. You flip this: show them that the best sites are built by founders who *understand their business*, not by founders who *know Figma* — and that AI bridges that gap.

### HOOK
- **Visual (first 2 seconds):** A split screen: left side shows a founder confused in Figma, right side shows the same founder *talking* to an AI agent. The AI generates the site from a simple conversation.  
- **Spoken first line:** *“You don’t need to become a designer to build a great website. You just need to know what you want — and let the machine do the rest.”*  
- **Why it works:** Founder ICP often feel they *should* learn design tools but don’t have time. This hook releases that pressure and positions you as the “translator” — not a teacher, but an enabler.

### SCRIPT
1. **[0:00 – 0:04]** (Split screen: left = frustrated founder in Figma, right = same person talking to AI agent. Text overlay: “LEARN DESIGN? OR LEARN YOUR BUSINESS?”)  
   **Voiceover:** *“You don’t need to become a designer to build a great website. You just need to know what you want — and let the machine do the rest.”*  
2. **[0:04 – 0:12]** (Screen: AI agent asks “What’s your business? Who’s your client? What’s your offer?” Text overlay: “AI ASKS THE RIGHT QUESTIONS.”)  
   **Voiceover:** *“I built a site for a coach who couldn’t design a button. But he could explain his process. The AI turned his explanation into a full site — with a client flow, a pricing page, and a booking system.”*  
3. **[0:12 – 0:22]** (Talking head, calm. Text overlay: “THE BEST SITES COME FROM CLARITY, NOT COMPLEXITY.”)  
   **Voiceover:** *“The AI didn’t need to know what ‘margin’ or ‘padding’ meant. It just needed to understand the founder’s intent. ‘I want people to book me after they read this.’ That’s the only prompt that matters.”*  
4. **[0:22 – 0:30]** (Screen: the final site — simple, clean, high-converting. Text overlay: “ONE CONVERSATION. ONE SITE.”)  
   **Voiceover:** *“The result? A site that looks like it cost $10k, but took one conversation to build. Because the architecture was already in the founder’s head — the AI just drew it.”*  
5. **[0:30 – 0:39]** (Talking head, slight smile. Text overlay: “YOU DON’T HIRE A DESIGNER. YOU HIRE A TRANSLATOR.”)  
   **Voiceover:** *“That’s what I do. I don’t teach you Figma. I teach you how to talk to your business — and then build the site that speaks back.”*  
6. **[0:39 – 0:47]** (CTA screen: “Comment ‘PRICING’ to see how your business translates into a website — for free.”)  
   **Voiceover:** *“If you’ve ever thought ‘I should just learn design,’ comment ‘PRICING’ — I’ll show you the better path.”*

**Takeaway:** The best website isn’t designed by a founder who knows tools — it’s built by a founder who knows their business.  
**CTA:** **PRICING**

---

### Why these concepts work for your brand:
- **Each hook** is a direct challenge to a founder’s belief (“homepage = business,” “more comments = better,” “I need to learn design”).  
- **Each script** teaches something genuinely useful (system thinking, feedback compression, intent over tool knowledge) and positions you as the authority who *builds* the solution, not just talks about it.  
- **Each CTA** is soft, curiosity-driven, and lead-gen — never a hard sell.

You can film these in one sitting with a simple split of talking head + screen recording. No hype, no jargon — just calm, confident expertise.
